title: Why go to all the trouble Angels 3I etc
display_title: Why go to all the trouble Angels 3I etc
wikitext:
[]: Why go to all the trouble? (Angels' 3I, etc.)             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Mar 09, 2008 6:26 pm I was reading the Third Impact section (http://evageeks.org/FGP/Third_Impact) in the Evageeks wiki and looked at what would have happened if the Angel's had started 3I. It states that they would have used an Anti-AT-Field to wipe out all humans. The Angel's did this, to a smaller degree, during Second Impact. They made everyone near Antarctica into LCL. So, if Seele wanted the human race to be one, why did they go to all the trouble stopping the Angels from reaching Adam and start Third Impact? If they had just let the Angels reach Adam and start their own 3I, Seele, without having done any work, would have won in their aim by making everyone into LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Mar 09, 2008 6:47 pm See, IF (a very big if) Gendo Ikari didn't want to resurrect Yui and followed Seele's plans/orders, then, obviously, it would've happened, with a few opponents of the version. But since Cmdr. Ikari wanted to be with Yui Ikari again, he didn't want all the human population to be one. Put basically, Cmdr. Ikari wanted to create his own version of Instrumentality, so he had to prevent the Angels from initiating Seele's version of Third Impact. Otherwise, it's just how the anime went. And how Anno wanted it to turn out. Anyway, even if Gendo did succeed in pulling off his own plan, then the anime would be extremely different from what we know now. Furthermore, Seele didn't want to follow the Angels' version of Instrumentality. See, all the three beings wanted their own version of Instrumentality. Seele, Ikari and the Angels' all had their own versions, so they would have to prevent each other from doing so.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 09, 2008 8:02 pm the ANgels would use Adam to reduce everyone to LCL, thus killing them, and the ANgels would dominate the planet. This would not result in all human souls fusing, the LCL sea is just the physical aspect of that. Seele wanted to united Lilith with Adam, which would also use an Anti A.T. Field to reduce everyone to LCL, but ultimately that wasn't the goal. Er, in simpler terms: The goal wasn't the "Sea of LCL", so much as "that giant ball of glowing red dots that Lilith had, each one a human soul".

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Mar 09, 2008 8:15 pm SEELE wanted everyone to return to our first mother, Lilith, and the origin of our souls, to "evolve" into a higher being when each soul would "complement" the other...like a HUGE jigsaw puzzle. The LCL Sea is basically all of our bodies turned into the basic molecules which it is composed from... Now...if we have a Universal Instrumentality...where Angels AND Human souls would complement each other in a fusion of Lilith and Adam...lol that would be interestingg to think about...the FAR would have a problem if that Megaorganism went around absorbing other Angels and Lilim civilizations...but that's a big tangent. And now, the bottom line. Like what V said, it's our Instrumentality versus Theirs.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 09, 2008 8:47 pm Not exactly: arguably the Angels aren't smart enough to do Instrumentality on their own. Which is to say: Instrumentality is not the "natural goal" of all lifeforms human or Angel to achieve a nirvana-transcendance of sorts. "Instrumentality" is pulling ourselves up by the bootstraps and making the unintended union of Adam and Lilith. If Lilith never arrived on Earth, the Angels would have just continued in a bizarre Adam-based ecology, never achieving "Instrumentality". The human version of "Third Impact" (well, Seele's version) involves Instrumentality, but the Angel-version of Third Impact does not. The Angel version of Third Impact is just "wipe out all the humans and reset the biosphere" Earth is the only planet where (purely by accident) both a Lilith-type and an Adam-type Seed of Life landed. Seele found out how they could jerry-rig the process into them forcing humanity on the path to godhood.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 09, 2008 9:13 pm V wrote: "Instrumentality" is pulling ourselves up by the bootstraps and making the unintended union of Adam and Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 09, 2008 11:04 pm V wrote: Not exactly: arguably the Angels aren't smart enough to do Instrumentality on their own.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:09 am @ V's Clarifications: Oh...forgotten that Adam was fused into Lilith. My bad. @Reichu: Whatever Adam was "bleeding" is...I'm assuming...an Angel's LCL. Ornette wrote: V wrote: Earth is the only planet where (purely by accident) both a Lilith-type and an Adam-type Seed of Life landed.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:24 am TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: @Reichu: Whatever Adam was "bleeding" is...I'm assuming...an Angel's LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:52 am Reichu wrote: I always thought the "fruit" dichotomy was contrived and stupid...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 8:09 am I'm sure if you look hard enough, you can find me sardonically complaining about this stuff in threads dating to years past.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 10, 2008 6:33 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Seele, Ikari and the Angels' all had their own versions, so they would have to prevent each other from doing so.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 6:36 pm Reichu wrote: idea arises from the idea that there are two "fruits" that, when combined, make a "god", and this system wouldn't seem to work quite as well if other Seeds with other fruits were out there. Probably more to it than that. I always thought the "fruit" dichotomy was contrived and stupid (HURR wuts a froot of know-lej nayway?), so I'm partial to "more than two types of Seeds" myself.
